seo.title: Web Analytics implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Web Analytics projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical roadmap for deploying privacy-first web analytics. It focuses on ensuring data integrity, regulatory compliance, and infrastructure stability while avoiding common implementation pitfalls like ad-blocker interference and PII leakage.

items.title: IP Anonymization Verification
items.description: Confirm that IP addresses are hashed with a rotating salt or truncated before database insertion to ensure GDPR compliance.

items.title: Cookie-less Configuration Audit
items.description: Verify the analytics script does not write to 'document.cookie' or 'localStorage' to bypass the requirement for a cookie consent banner.

items.title: DNT Header Respect
items.description: Check that the tracking script honors the browser's 'Do Not Track' (DNT) signal by disabling data collection when active.

items.title: PII Leakage Scan
items.description: Inspect outgoing network requests to ensure that email addresses, names, or auth tokens are not included in URL parameters or event properties.

items.title: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
items.description: Ensure a signed DPA is in place with the analytics vendor if using a hosted service like Plausible or Fathom.

items.title: Asynchronous Script Loading
items.description: Use 'async' or 'defer' attributes on the script tag to prevent the analytics library from blocking the critical rendering path.

items.title: Custom Domain/Proxy Setup
items.description: Configure a reverse proxy (e.g., via Nginx or Vercel Rewrites) to serve the script from your own domain to minimize ad-blocker interference.

items.title: SPA Route Change Tracking
items.description: For React/Next.js/Vue apps, verify that page views trigger on router events rather than just initial page load.

items.title: Bundle Size Audit
items.description: Measure the impact of the analytics library on the total JS bundle size; ensure it stays under 5KB for privacy-focused tools.

items.title: SSR Compatibility Check
items.description: Ensure the tracking script is only executed in the browser context to prevent 'window is not defined' errors during server-side rendering.

items.title: Naming Convention Standardization
items.description: Enforce an 'object_action' naming convention (e.g., 'button_click') across all custom events to ensure report consistency.

items.title: Environment Separation
items.description: Use different Site IDs or API keys for 'development', 'staging', and 'production' to prevent test data from polluting production metrics.

items.title: Internal Traffic Filtering
items.description: Exclude traffic from internal IP addresses or specific local storage flags to prevent team activity from skewing data.

items.title: Bot and Crawler Exclusion
items.description: Verify that the tool automatically filters out common search engine bots and headless browsers from the dashboard.

items.title: Outbound Link Tracking
items.description: Test that clicks on external links are correctly captured before the browser navigates away from the page.

items.title: Database Retention Policies
items.description: Configure automated data pruning or TTL (Time To Live) settings to prevent the database from exceeding disk capacity.

items.title: SSL/TLS Enforcement
items.description: Ensure the analytics endpoint is served exclusively over HTTPS to protect data in transit.

items.title: Automated Backups
items.description: Establish a daily backup schedule for the analytics database (PostgreSQL/ClickHouse) to an off-site S3-compatible bucket.

items.title: Resource Monitoring
items.description: Set up alerts for high CPU or memory usage on the analytics container to prevent tracking downtime during traffic spikes.

items.title: Health Check Endpoint
items.description: Implement and monitor a '/health' or '/ping' endpoint to verify the availability of the tracking server.

items.title: Dashboard Access Control
items.description: Verify that dashboard access is restricted to authorized team members via SSO or 2FA.

items.title: Conversion Goal Verification
items.description: Manually trigger a conversion (e.g., signup or purchase) and verify it appears in the dashboard within the expected latency window.

items.title: API Export Functionality
items.description: Test the API endpoint for data retrieval to ensure automated reports or internal BI tools can ingest the analytics data.

items.title: Cross-Domain Linker Configuration
items.description: If tracking across multiple subdomains, verify that the user session is preserved across transitions.

items.title: Alerting for Traffic Drops
items.description: Set up notifications for significant, unexpected drops in traffic which might indicate a broken tracking script.
